label: ∅ | be | dervish
sentence: Once, there was a wandering dervish known for his deep faith and devotion.

label: he | lose | desert
sentence: He was lost in the desert, starving and desperate for food.

label: he | spot | sack
sentence: As he walked along an old path, he spotted an empty fruit sack left by someone who had passed before.

label: he | pick | it
sentence: Gratefully, he picked it up, slung it over his shoulder, and prayed aloud, “Thank God for giving a starving man an empty fruit sack,” before continuing on.

label: he | find | bow
sentence: A little farther ahead, he found an old hunting bow on the ground, but its string was broken.

label: he | place | bow
sentence: He placed the bow into his sack and again gave thanks aloud, “Thank God for giving a starving man a hunting bow with a broken string.”

label: he | see | tree
sentence: Further along the road, he saw a dead tree with no fruit.

label: he | break | branch
sentence: He broke off some dry branches and added them to his sack, once more praying aloud, “Thank God for guiding a starving man to a dead fruit tree.”

label: he | discover | pot
sentence: As he walked on, he discovered a battered cooking pot lying on the ground.

label: he | put | it
sentence: After blowing off the dust, he put it into his sack and thanked God aloud again, “Thank God for giving a starving man a dented old pot full of dust.”

label: he | find | hook
sentence: Later, he found a lone fishing hook but no pole to go with it.

label: he | pick | hook
sentence: He picked up the hook, dropped it into his sack, and reiterated his gratitude loudly, “Thank God for giving a starving man a fishing hook without a fishing pole.”

label: path | bring | he
sentence: After many days of wandering, the path finally brought him to a vast river, so wide he could not see the opposite shore.

label: dervish | sink | knee
sentence: The old dervish sank to his knees by the water’s edge and prayed loudly, “Thank God for leading a starving man to a river so wide that crossing it seems impossible.”

label: he | tie | hook
sentence: Then, with his calm spirit and faith, he tied the fishing hook to the broken bowstring, using the bow as a makeshift fishing rod.

label: he | build | fire
sentence: Using the dry branches, he built a fire and cooked the fish he caught in the old pot, finally nourishing himself with a meal born of patience and trust.
